title: Spelta amylea
taxonomy: Spelta amylea is commonly known as spelt, and it is a type of ancient grain. It is classified as a hexaploid wheat, meaning it has six sets of chromosomes. The scientific name Spelta amylea is often considered a synonym for Triticum spelta, which is the most widely accepted classification for this species. Triticum spelta is a member of the Poaceae family and is closely related to other wheat species. Spelta amylea's classification as a hexaploid wheat makes it well-suited for certain agricultural purposes, such as producing high-protein flour.
